unsorted.command: did_imputation(data = panel, yname = "Y", gname = "Ei", tname = "t", idname = "unit", wname = "w", horizon = c(2, 0), cluster_var = "unit")

sparse.command: did_imputation(data = panel, yname = "Y", gname = "Ei", tname = "t", idname = "unit", wname = "w", horizon = c(0, 2), cluster_var = "unit")

absent.command: did_imputation(data = panel, yname = "Y", gname = "Ei", tname = "t", idname = "unit", wname = "w", horizon = c(0, 3), cluster_var = "unit")

duplicate.command: did_imputation(data = panel, yname = "Y", gname = "Ei", tname = "t", idname = "unit", wname = "w", horizon = c(0, 0), cluster_var = "unit")
duplicate.error_message: Can't assign to the same column twice in the same query (duplicates detected).

negative.command: did_imputation(data = panel, yname = "Y", gname = "Ei", tname = "t", idname = "unit", wname = "w", horizon = -1, cluster_var = "unit")
negative.error_message: Some items of .SDcols are not column names: [zz000wtr]

empty.command: did_imputation(data = panel, yname = "Y", gname = "Ei", tname = "t", idname = "unit", wname = "w", horizon = integer(0), cluster_var = "unit")

all_horizons_true.command: did_imputation(data = panel, yname = "Y", gname = "Ei", tname = "t", idname = "unit", wname = "w", horizon = TRUE, cluster_var = "unit")
